usage_breakdown.label: Heating & hot water
tips: Insulate pipes and use timer on water heater
Shorter showers save hot water energy

usage_breakdown.label: Kitchen (cooking, fridge, dishwasher)
tips: Use lid on pots – cooks faster, uses less energy

usage_breakdown.label: Electronics & standby
tips: Unplug devices on standby – can save €50–100/year

usage_breakdown.label: Lighting
tips: Switch to LED bulbs – 80% less energy
Turn off lights in unused rooms

usage_breakdown.label: Washing & drying
tips: Air-dry instead of tumble dryer when possible

usage_breakdown.label: Cooling & ventilation
tips: Use fans instead of AC where possible

usage_breakdown.label: Other
tips: Review all appliances for energy rating

usage_breakdown.label: EV charging & mobility
tips: Consider solar-compatible charger if you move to a house

why_explanation: We recommend a 5.04 kWp system because your estimated consumption is about 4,200 kWh/year, your roof profile (pitched south) appears suitable for roughly 4,644 kWh/year of production, and this size balances self-consumption with export for your goals (lower bill). We have not assumed a battery unless your goal requires it.
why_recommend: **Lower electricity bill** → Rooftop PV sized around annual usage and daytime consumption
A battery is NOT automatically recommended for bill savings – compare the three packages below.
A 5.04 kWp system targets your annual usage and daytime consumption pattern.
Estimated payback of 8.4 years – within typical German range.
